mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth signals the underlying pace of economic expansion. When GDPC1 is rising steadily, corporate earnings improve and equity prices follow over the subsequent month. This mechanism is always active because trend growth is the baseline driver of equity returns in expansionary periods.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year rank IC between GDPC1 growth and forward 20-day returns is negative or below 0.02, or mean forward return in rising GDP quarters is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate breaches 6%, it signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, dragging down equity prices over the next quarter. The threshold is set at 6% because this level historically marks the onset of recessionary conditions.
failure_condition: More than 50% of UNRATE > 6.0 trigger instances produce positive 60-day forward returns, or average return gap between triggered and non-triggered periods is not negative with p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: High real interest rates, measured as the federal funds rate minus year-over-year CPI inflation, compress equity valuations by raising discount rates. The interaction of high nominal rates and low inflation produces especially negative forward returns as the real rate burden increases.
failure_condition: Composite real-rate signal has rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns in high real-rate periods are not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production over three months signals weakening manufacturing activity and impending recession. When the year-over-year growth rate of INDPRO turns negative, forward equity returns over the next quarter are depressed as earnings expectations drop.
failure_condition: More than 60% of INDPRO decline episodes are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns, or average return in triggered windows is not negative with p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly growth in retail and food services sales indicates robust consumer demand that boosts near-term corporate revenues. When the month-over-month growth rate of RSAFS exceeds 0.5%, the S&P 500 tends to rise over the following week as consumption surprises lift market sentiment.
failure_condition: More than 40% of threshold triggers are followed by negative 5-day returns, or average return after trigger is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with falling interest rates reflects accommodative monetary policy that stimulates construction and related industries. The joint acceleration of HOUST and deceleration of FEDFUNDS creates a powerful positive signal for equity markets over the next month as economic activity broadens.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction signal (HOUST up & FEDFUNDS down) stays below 0.03 over any 3-year window, or direction of average return contradicts the implied sign.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output, which correlates with higher corporate earnings and positive equity returns. This trend signal is always active because industrial production trend is a fundamental driver of economic growth in normal times.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year rank IC of INDPRO growth and forward 20-day returns is below 0.02, or mean return in periods of positive growth is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 3% and the federal funds rate is above 4%, the economy is in a stagflationary regime where both inflation and tight monetary policy squeeze corporate margins and valuations. This combined condition reliably predicts negative equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: More than 50% of trigger instances produce positive 60-day returns, or average return in triggered windows is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).
